#: JSON Model generated from a JSON Schema with json-schema-utils

?$schema.#: Ignored. Can be set to get completions, validations and documentation in some editors.

?alias.#: Aliases that will get assigned when the deployment is `READY` and the target is `production`. The client needs to make a `GET` request to its API to ensure the assignment

?build.#: An object containing another object with information to be passed to the Build Process

?env.#: An object containing the deployment's environment variable names and values to be passed to Builds. Secrets can be referenced by prefixing the value with `@`

?buildCommand.#: The build command for this project. When `null` is used this value will be automatically detected

?builds.#: A list of build descriptions whose src references valid source files.

?config.#: Optionally, an object including arbitrary metadata to be passed to the Builder

?src.#: A glob expression or pathname. If more than one file is resolved, one build will be created per matched file. It can include `*` and `**`

_use.#: An npm module to be installed by the build process. It can include a semver compatible version (e.g.: `@org/proj@1`)

?cleanUrls.#: When set to `true`, all HTML files and Serverless Functions will have their extension removed. When visiting a path that ends with the extension, a 308 response will redirect the client to the extensionless path.

?crons.#: An array of cron jobs that should be created for production Deployments.

?devCommand.#: The dev command for this project. When `null` is used this value will be automatically detected

?env.#: An object containing the deployment's environment variable names and values. Secrets can be referenced by prefixing the value with `@`

?framework.#: The framework that is being used for this project. When `null` is used no framework is selected

?functions.#: An object describing custom options for your Serverless Functions. Each key must be glob pattern that matches the paths of the Serverless Functions you would like to customize (like `api/*.js` or `api/test.js`).

?excludeFiles.#: A glob pattern to match files that should be excluded from your Serverless Function. If you’re using a Community Runtime, the behavior might vary.

?includeFiles.#: A glob pattern to match files that should be included in your Serverless Function. If you’re using a Community Runtime, the behavior might vary.

?maxDuration.#: An integer defining how long your Serverless Function should be allowed to run on every request in seconds (between 1 and the maximum limit of your plan).

?memory.#: An integer defining the memory your Serverless Function should be provided with (between 128 and 3008).

?runtime.#: The npm package name of a Runtime, including its version

?deploymentEnabled.#: Specifies the branches that will not trigger an auto-deployment when committing to them. Any non specified branch is `true` by default.

?autoAlias.#: When set to `false`, Vercel for GitHub will not deploy the given project regardless of the GitHub app being installed.

?autoJobCancelation.#: When set to `false`, Vercel for GitHub will always build pushes in sequence without cancelling a build for the most recent commit.

?enabled.#: When set to false, Vercel for GitHub will not apply the alias upon merge.

?silent.#: When set to `true`, Vercel for GitHub will stop commenting on pull requests and commits.

?has.#: An array of requirements that are needed to match

_type.#: The type of request element to check

_value.#: A regular expression used to match the value. Named groups can be used in the destination

?value.#: A regular expression used to match the value. Named groups can be used in the destination

_key.#: The name of the element contained in the particular type

_type.#: The type of request element to check

?missing.#: An array of requirements that are needed to match

_type.#: The type of request element to check

_value.#: A regular expression used to match the value. Named groups can be used in the destination

?value.#: A regular expression used to match the value. Named groups can be used in the destination

_key.#: The name of the element contained in the particular type

_type.#: The type of request element to check

_headers.#: An array of key/value pairs representing each response header.

_source.#: A pattern that matches each incoming pathname (excluding querystring)

?installCommand.#: The install command for this project. When `null` is used this value will be automatically detected

?outputDirectory.#: The output directory of the project. When `null` is used this value will be automatically detected

?public.#: Whether a deployment's source and logs are available publicly

?permanent.#: A boolean to toggle between permanent and temporary redirect. When `true`, the status code is `308`. When `false` the status code is `307`.

_destination.#: A location destination defined as an absolute pathname or external URL.

_source.#: A pattern that matches each incoming pathname (excluding querystring).

?regions.#: An array of the regions the deployment's Serverless Functions should be deployed to

?scope.#: This property determines the scope (user or team) under which the project will be deployed by Vercel CLI.

?trailingSlash.#: When `false`, visiting a path that ends with a forward slash will respond with a `308` status code and redirect to the path without the trailing slash.
